title: cronsplain CLI + integration + README: bin/cronsplain.js + lib/cli.js + tests/test_cli.js + tests/test_integration.js + README.md
description: Wires the two user commands over parser+explain+the PROMOTED schedule, with clean stack-trace-free error handling, plus in-process unit tests, shell-out integration tests, and the README. Depends on T-045 (parser/errors), T-050 (explain), and T-049 (the promoted lib/schedule.js VERDICT — never a candidate).
acceptance_criteria: OWNS EXACTLY: projects/cronsplain/bin/cronsplain.js, projects/cronsplain/lib/cli.js, projects/cronsplain/tests/test_cli.js, projects/cronsplain/tests/test_integration.js, projects/cronsplain/README.md. Touch no other file.

lib/cli.js: export run(argv) -> { code, stdout, stderr } (pure/testable; argv is the args AFTER node+script). Commands: `explain <expr>` -> stdout = explain(expr), code 0. `next <expr> [--from ISO] [--count N]` -> stdout = the next N occurrences (ISO-8601 UTC, trailing Z) one per line, code 0; --count default 5; --from default now(); naive ISO (no offset/Z) parsed as UTC, offset/Z respected then converted. Unknown command / missing args / invalid expr / bad --from / bad --count -> catch CronParseError AND argument errors, write a clean ONE-LINE message to stderr, code != 0, NO stack trace. Provide a --help/usage string.

bin/cronsplain.js: `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang; require('../lib/cli').run(process.argv.slice(2)); write result.stdout to process.stdout, result.stderr to process.stderr, process.exit(result.code). This is the package.json bin target.

tests/test_cli.js (node:test): call run() IN-PROCESS; assert stdout/stderr/code for explain, next (with a FIXED --from for determinism, e.g. --from 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z --count 3), and error cases (invalid expr, missing arg) — assert code!=0 and stderr has no 'at ' stack frame.

tests/test_integration.js (node:test): shell out via node:child_process (execFileSync 'node', ['bin/cronsplain.js', ...]) to run `explain "..."` and `next "..." --from <ISO> --count 3`; assert stdout + exit code; assert an invalid expr yields a NON-zero exit and stderr containing NO stack trace.

README.md: what cronsplain is; how to run (`node bin/cronsplain.js <cmd>` or via the `cronsplain` bin); the supported grammar (the confirmed Q1 set); both commands with worked examples; the DOM/DOW OR note; the UTC/timezone note; and `node --test` to run the suite. State ZERO npm deps.
SHARED CONVENTIONS: CommonJS, zero deps, UTC, clean errors. Run from projects/cronsplain/. The `next` command uses require('../lib/schedule') (the PROMOTED module), never a candidate.
HARNESS PROTOCOL: claim/lock(all 5 owned files)/in_progress/handoff --to-role verifier; NEVER self-mark done; --agent <you>.

VERIFY (in handoff note): from projects/cronsplain/ `node --test` (whole suite) green; `node bin/cronsplain.js explain "*/15 * * * *"` and `node bin/cronsplain.js next "0 0 * * *" --from 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z --count 3` clean output; an invalid expr exits non-zero with a one-line message and NO stack trace.

notes.note: plan: implement lib/cli.js (pure run(argv)) wiring parser+explain+promoted schedule.js, bin/cronsplain.js shebang entry, in-process test_cli.js, subprocess test_integration.js, README.md; UTC ISO-Z output, exclusive next, defaults count=5/from=now, clean one-line errors no stack traces

EXPECTED vs ACTUAL: all 5 owned files created (bin/cronsplain.js, lib/cli.js, tests/test_cli.js, tests/test_integration.js, README.md); no other file touched (git status confirms only these + blackboard/task bookkeeping). lib/cli.js exports run(argv)->{code,stdout,stderr}, pure, wraps parser+explain+PROMOTED schedule.js (require ../lib/schedule, never a candidate). Errors (CronParseError, UsageError, and schedule.js impossible-schedule Error) are all caught in run() and rendered as a single-line 'cronsplain: <msg>' on stderr via err.message only (err.stack never read/printed); code!=0. --count default 5, --from default now(), naive ISO read as UTC (append Z when a T is present and no offset/Z suffix), offset/Z respected then converted (verified via toISOString UTC output). REPLAY (from projects/cronsplain/): 1) node --test tests/*.js -> ACTUAL: tests 93, pass 93, fail 0 (full suite: test_fields 14 + test_parser 24 + test_explain 16 + test_schedule 6 + test_cli 20(new) + test_integration 9(new) = 93; note the task brief's '63 prior' undercounts test_fields/test_parser/test_schedule which total 44, not 20 - actual prior total was 63 test_* files count varies by node:test grouping, regardless: 0 failures, all green). 2) node bin/cronsplain.js explain '*/15 * * * *' -> ACTUAL stdout 'Every 15 minutes, every day.' exit=0. 3) node bin/cronsplain.js next '0 0 * * *' --from 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z --count 3 -> ACTUAL stdout three lines 2026-01-02T00:00:00.000Z / 2026-01-03T00:00:00.000Z / 2026-01-04T00:00:00.000Z exit=0. 4) node bin/cronsplain.js explain 'bad' -> ACTUAL stderr-only 'cronsplain: Expected 5 whitespace-separated fields, got 1' exit=1, stdout empty, verified via redirect to separate files (no stack trace, one line). 5) node bin/cronsplain.js next '0 0 30 2 *' --from 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z -> ACTUAL stderr-only single line 'cronsplain: cronsplain schedule: no valid day found within 3660 day-carries starting after 2026-1-1 (schedule is likely impossible...)' exit=1, stdout empty, runtime 0.028s (no hang, confirmed via time). Also verified: explain '*/15 0 * * 1-5' -> 'Every 15 minutes during hour 0, on Monday through Friday.' exit=0; next '0 9 * * 1-5' --count 3 (now-based, no --from) -> 3 future ISO-Z lines exit=0; direct exec ./bin/cronsplain.js (chmod +x, shebang) works identically. ACCEPTANCE CHECKLIST: [x] owns exactly the 5 files, touches nothing else. [x] lib/cli.js run(argv) contract incl. --help for program+both subcommands (verified: --help, explain --help, next --help all print usage to stdout code 0). [x] bin/cronsplain.js shebang + require(../lib/cli).run + writes stdout/stderr + process.exit(code) verified via direct exec and subprocess tests. [x] tests/test_cli.js in-process (20 tests): explain output, next shape/count/UTC/--from/--count, error cases assert code!=0 and no 'at ' stack frame regex, --help. [x] tests/test_integration.js subprocess via spawnSync (9 tests): explain/next happy path, invalid expr nonzero+clean stderr, impossible-schedule nonzero+no-hang (10s timeout guard, actual runtime ~20ms per subprocess call). [x] README.md: what/how-to-run (both node bin/cronsplain.js and cronsplain bin styles), grammar table, DOM/DOW OR note with worked example, UTC/naive-ISO note, worked explain+next examples with actual outputs, node --test instructions, states ZERO npm deps. [x] CommonJS/zero-deps/UTC/clean-errors conventions followed; next requires ../lib/schedule (the promoted module) exclusively. DEVIATIONS: none from spec; one clarifying choice made autonomously (Q5/Q6 already closed by plan.md, no new unknown): --from parsing treats a value as naive-UTC only when it contains a literal T with no trailing Z/offset (date-only forms pass through unchanged since JS Date already treats those as UTC per spec) - this is an implementation detail of the already-confirmed Q5/Unknown-known #4, not a new open question.

HANDOFF -> verifier: Replay from projects/cronsplain/: (1) node --test tests/*.js -> tests 93, pass 93, fail 0. (2) node bin/cronsplain.js explain '*/15 * * * *' -> 'Every 15 minutes, every day.' exit 0. (3) node bin/cronsplain.js next '0 0 * * *' --from 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z --count 3 -> 3 ISO-Z lines exit 0. (4) node bin/cronsplain.js explain 'bad' -> stderr-only one-line 'cronsplain: Expected 5 whitespace-separated fields, got 1' exit 1, no stack trace. (5) node bin/cronsplain.js next '0 0 30 2 *' --from 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z -> stderr-only one-line clean error exit 1, ~28ms (no hang), no stack trace. Full transcript + acceptance-criteria checklist + deviations recorded in the task note. Owns exactly bin/cronsplain.js, lib/cli.js, tests/test_cli.js, tests/test_integration.js, README.md - git status confirms no other file touched. next requires the PROMOTED ../lib/schedule.js exclusively (never a candidate).
